description_long: This is a vintage freestanding oven and stove combination, featuring a robust metal body with a white and gray enamel finish. The front section includes a large oven door with a metal handle, a smaller compartment below, and a stovetop with four prominent gas burners. Above the cooking surface, there is a two-tiered metal rack for storage or drying, supported by a sturdy frame. The side views reveal the oven's boxy, utilitarian construction, with visible hinges and latches, while the back is solid and reinforced with metal supports. The overall design is functional and industrial, suitable for heavy-duty use in an urban or workshop setting.
description: A vintage metal oven and stove with four burners, a large oven door, and a two-tiered metal rack above. The body is finished in white and gray enamel with sturdy, utilitarian features.
description_view_0: Front view showing the oven door, lower compartment, four gas burners, and the full two-tiered rack above.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the depth of the oven, the side panel, and the profile of the rack and burners.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a solid black metal back with structural supports and no visible controls or openings.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the side panel, the rack, and the burners from a different angle, with visible hinges and latches.
attribute_control_panel_design: No dedicated control panel is visible; burner controls appear to be mechanical knobs located on the front face below the burners, with no digital displays or touch elements. The design is minimal and functional, with no apparent labeling or decorative features.
attribute_door_handle_type: The oven door handle is a horizontally mounted, cylindrical metal bar with a simple, protruding design. It appears robust and functional, with no visible ergonomic padding or decorative elements.

license_info.creator_display_name: Museum of Engineering and Technology, Krakow
